{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {

    "R1.5": 1,
    "RX.1": 5,

  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The protocol description has several internal inconsistencies, including the mechanism for linearizable reads and writes.",
    "R1.5": "No structural feedback was provided on how to reorganize the protocol description.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty is disclosed in the evaluation claims that are not supported by what is reported.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with one drift, as the deployment paragraph is an anecdote rather than evidence.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors and fabrications, specifically the overclaiming of linearizable reads and writes without a safety argument.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability are not provided for every non-cosmetic change in the protocol description."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "R1.5": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.9,

  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.2: The protocol description has several internal inconsistencies, including the mechanism for linearizable reads and writes.
rationale.R1.5: No structural feedback was provided on how to reorganize the protocol description.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty is disclosed in the evaluation claims that are not supported by what is reported.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift, as the deployment paragraph is an anecdote rather than evidence.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors and fabrications, specifically the overclaiming of linearizable reads and writes without a safety argument.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are not provided for every non-cosmetic change in the protocol description.
